description: This domain contains data which has Cornerstone Learning data

tables.name: User_csod
tables.description: This table contains essential user information for employees within the Cornerstone Learning system, including identifiers, contact details, and organizational structure.

columns.name: userId
columns.description: Unique identifier for users in the Cornerstone system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of user records
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format to ensure it meets VARCHAR standards
metadata.business_description: A unique identifier assigned to each user for tracking their learning performance and activities within the CSOD platform.

columns.name: userName
columns.description: Column representing the username of the user in the system.

business_rules: Must be unique within the system
Can be null if the user has not set a username
data_quality_checks: Check for duplicates in the userName column
Validate format to ensure it meets username criteria
metadata.business_description: The username is used to identify users within the Cornerstone Learn platform and is essential for user authentication and tracking learning activities.

columns.name: fullName
columns.description: The complete name of the user as recorded in the system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should not contain special characters
data_quality_checks: Check for leading or trailing spaces
Validate format to ensure it contains at least a first and last name
metadata.business_description: This column represents the full name of the user, which is essential for identifying individuals in reports and dashboards.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Column representing the email address of the user.

business_rules: Must be a valid email format
Can be null if the user has not provided an email
data_quality_checks: Validate email format
Check for duplicates within the user base
metadata.business_description: Email address used for communication and notifications related to learning activities.

columns.name: managerId
columns.description: Identifier for the manager of the user, represented as a string.

business_rules: Can be null if the user does not have a manager
Should reference a valid userId in the User_csod table if not null
data_quality_checks: Check for valid userId references in the User_csod table
Ensure that if present, the managerId corresponds to an existing manager
metadata.business_description: This column represents the unique identifier of the user's manager, which can be used to establish reporting relationships within the organization.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by managerId to analyze users under specific managers
Filter by userStatus to see active/inactive users under a manager

columns.name: managerName
columns.description: The name of the manager associated with the user.

business_rules: Can be null if the user does not have a manager assigned
Should reflect the current manager's name
data_quality_checks: Ensure the name is formatted correctly (e.g., no special characters)
Check for consistency with the managerId column
metadata.business_description: This column represents the name of the user's manager, which is important for understanding reporting structures within the organization.

columns.name: position
columns.description: The job title or role of the user within the organization.

business_rules: Can be null if the user does not have a defined position
Should accurately reflect the user's current job title
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the position is a valid job title recognized by the organization
Check for consistency in naming conventions (e.g., avoid abbreviations)
metadata.business_description: This column represents the position held by the user, which is essential for understanding their role in the organization and for reporting on employee performance and compliance.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by specific positions to analyze performance metrics
Filter by division to see position distribution
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY position for performance analysis

columns.name: division
columns.description: Column representing the division within the organization to which a user belongs.

business_rules: Can be null
Should reflect the current division of the user
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are consistent with predefined divisions
Check for typos or incorrect entries
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the specific division of the company that the user is associated with, which can be used to analyze user performance and compliance within different divisions.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status within division

columns.name: location
columns.description: Column representing the geographical location of the user.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the user's current location
data_quality_checks: Validate against known location formats
Check for consistency with user profiles
metadata.business_description: Indicates the physical or virtual location where the user is based, which can impact training and compliance activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status and location

columns.name: userStatus
columns.description: Indicates the current status of the user within the system.

business_rules: Can be null
Valid statuses include 'Active', 'Inactive', 'On Leave'
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are one of the predefined statuses
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents whether a user is active, inactive, or on leave, which is crucial for understanding user engagement and compliance with learning activities.
aggregation_suggestions: PERCENTAGE of active vs inactive users

columns.name: lastLogindate
columns.description: The date when the user last logged into the system.

business_rules: Can be null if the user has never logged in
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date format is consistent
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents the most recent date that a user accessed their account, which is crucial for tracking user engagement and compliance with learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status to see active vs inactive users
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of users who logged in within a specific timeframe
MAX last login date to find the most recent activity

columns.name: startDate
columns.description: The date when the user started their learning activities.

business_rules: Can be null if the user has not started any learning activities
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for future dates
metadata.business_description: This column represents the start date of the user's learning journey within the Cornerstone system, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of users by start date
Average start date for compliance activities

columns.description: Column userId

columns.name: userName
columns.description: Column userName

columns.name: fullName
columns.description: Column fullName

columns.name: email
columns.description: Column email

columns.name: managerId
columns.description: Column managerId

columns.name: managerName
columns.description: Column managerName

columns.name: position
columns.description: Column position

columns.name: division
columns.description: Column division

columns.name: location
columns.description: Column location

metadata.data_lineage: Data is sourced from the Cornerstone Learning platform and is populated through regular data extraction processes.
metadata.data_retention: Data is retained for a period of 5 years to comply with organizational policies.
access_patterns: Frequent read access by managers and admins for reporting purposes
Occasional write access for updates to user information
metadata.business_purpose: The User_csod table serves as a foundational data source for generating insights into employee learning performance, enabling effective management and reporting within the organization.

key_relationships: Users are linked to their respective managers for hierarchical reporting
User data is associated with learning performance metrics for analysis
primary_use_cases: Creating reports on user engagement in learning programs
Analyzing user status and activity trends over time

semantic_description.description: The User_csod table serves as a comprehensive repository of user information within an organizational context, capturing essential details about employees for management and operational purposes.

key_columns.name: userId
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each user in the system
key_columns.business_significance: Acts as the primary key for user identification and data integrity across various applications

key_columns.name: userName
key_columns.description: Username used for system login and identification
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for user authentication and access control within the system

key_columns.name: email
key_columns.description: User's primary email address for communication
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for internal and external communication, as well as for notifications and alerts

key_columns.name: managerId
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for the user's manager
key_columns.business_significance: Facilitates the establishment of reporting relationships and organizational hierarchy

key_columns.name: userStatus
key_columns.description: Current employment status of the user (e.g., active, inactive)
key_columns.business_significance: Important for tracking employee lifecycle and resource allocation

key_columns.name: startDate
key_columns.description: Date when the user commenced employment
key_columns.business_significance: Useful for tenure analysis and understanding employee retention

semantic_description.table_purpose: To maintain a centralized record of user profiles, including personal details, employment status, and hierarchical relationships within the organization.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is integral to human resource management and organizational operations, providing a structured view of employee data that supports various functions such as performance management, reporting, and compliance. It enables the organization to manage its workforce effectively and maintain clear communication channels.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each user belongs to a specific division, allowing for departmental reporting and resource management
Each user can have multiple activity logs, enabling tracking of user engagement and system usage

performance_considerations: Indexing on userId and email for faster query performance
Monitoring for data growth to ensure optimal database performance

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

tables.name: Transcript_csod
tables.description: This table contains detailed records of employee learning activities, including completion status, scores, and timestamps for various training events.

columns.name: loID
columns.description: Identifier for learning objects in the Cornerstone Learn system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of the learning objects
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format to ensure it meets VARCHAR standards
metadata.business_description: This column represents the unique identifier for each learning object associated with employee training and compliance activities.

columns.name: userID
columns.description: Identifier for users in the system, representing each learner's unique account.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of user records
data_quality_checks: Check for duplicates in user records
Validate format to ensure it meets VARCHAR standards
metadata.business_description: A unique identifier assigned to each user participating in training activities, used to track their learning performance and compliance.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to analyze specific learner performance
Filter by trainingStatus to assess compliance

columns.name: isCompleted
columns.description: Indicates whether the training activity has been completed by the learner.

business_rules: Can be null
Values must be 'true' or 'false'
data_quality_checks: Check for valid boolean values
Ensure no unexpected values are present
metadata.business_description: This column represents the completion status of a training activity for an employee. A value of 'true' indicates that the training has been completed, while 'false' indicates it has not.

columns.name: isOverdue
columns.description: Indicates whether a training assignment is overdue based on the due date.

business_rules: Can be null
Values must be 'Yes' or 'No'
data_quality_checks: Check for valid values ('Yes', 'No')
Ensure no unexpected values are present
metadata.business_description: This column represents whether a training assignment has passed its due date, which is critical for tracking compliance and learner performance.

columns.name: isAssigned
columns.description: Indicates whether a training module is assigned to a user.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not assigned
Should reflect true/false values indicating assignment status
data_quality_checks: Validate that values are either 'true' or 'false'
Check for null values where assignment is expected
metadata.business_description: This column represents the assignment status of a training module to a user, helping to track which users have been assigned specific training.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see assignments per user

columns.name: score
columns.description: Column representing the performance score of a learner in the training module.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not completed
Should be a numeric value representing performance
data_quality_checks: Validate that the score is a numeric value
Check for any unexpected characters in the score
metadata.business_description: This score indicates the level of achievement or performance of an employee in their learning activities.

columns.name: success
columns.description: Indicates whether the learner successfully completed the training module.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not yet completed
Should reflect 'true' for successful completions and 'false' for failures
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are either 'true' or 'false' or null
Check for consistency with the isCompleted column
metadata.business_description: This column represents the success status of a learner in completing a training module, which is crucial for assessing employee learning performance.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by success status to analyze completion rates
Filter by userID to assess individual performance
aggregation_suggestions: PERCENTAGE of successful completions against total attempts

columns.name: timeSpent
columns.description: Duration of time spent by the user on a specific learning activity, recorded in a VARCHAR format representing a numeric value.

business_rules: Can be null if the user has not started the activity
Should be a valid numeric representation in string format
data_quality_checks: Validate that the value can be converted to a numeric type
Check for excessively high values that may indicate data entry errors
metadata.business_description: This column captures the amount of time a user has spent on a learning activity, which is crucial for analyzing user engagement and performance in training programs.

columns.name: satisfiedLate
columns.description: Indicates whether the learner was satisfied with the training completed after the due date.

business_rules: Can be null if the training was completed on time
Should reflect a binary satisfaction status (e.g., satisfied, not satisfied)
data_quality_checks: Check for valid satisfaction responses (e.g., 'Yes', 'No')
Ensure consistency with completion dates
metadata.business_description: This column captures the satisfaction level of learners who completed their training late, providing insights into learner experience and training effectiveness.
aggregation_suggestions: Percentage of satisfiedLate against total late completions

columns.name: trainingStatus
columns.description: Indicates the current status of the training for a user.

business_rules: Can be null if no training is assigned
Must reflect valid statuses such as 'Assigned', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'Overdue'
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are one of the predefined statuses
Check for null values only when no training is assigned
metadata.business_description: This column represents whether a training module is currently assigned, completed, overdue, or in progress for a user.

columns.name: cancellationDate
columns.description: Date when the training or learning activity was cancelled.

business_rules: Can be null if the training was not cancelled
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Check for valid date format
Ensure that cancellationDate is not later than completionDate
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date on which a training session or learning activity was cancelled by the user or the system.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see cancellations per user

columns.name: registrationDate
columns.description: The date when the user registered for the training or course.

business_rules: Can be null if the user has not registered for a training
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for future dates
Ensure consistency with other date fields
metadata.business_description: This column captures the date of registration for each training session, which is essential for tracking user engagement and compliance with training requirements.

columns.name: attemptStartDate
columns.description: The date when the learning attempt started, represented as a timestamp without time zone.

business_rules: Can be null if the attempt has not started
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the start date of a learner's attempt to complete a training module.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to analyze individual attempts
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY date for daily reports

columns.name: completionDate
columns.description: The date when the training or learning activity was completed by the user.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not completed
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., cannot be after dueDate)
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date on which a learner has completed a specific training module or activity, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance in learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see individual completion dates

columns.name: assignedDate
columns.description: The date when the training or learning activity was assigned to the user.

business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been assigned yet
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date on which a specific training or learning activity was assigned to an employee, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance in learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by assignedDate to analyze training assignments over time
Filter by userID to see assignments for specific employees
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY assignedDate for trend analysis

columns.name: dueDate
columns.description: The date by which a training or compliance activity must be completed.

business_rules: Can be null if the training is not time-sensitive
Must be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., registrationDate, completionDate)
metadata.business_description: This column represents the deadline for learners to complete their assigned training or compliance activities.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings due by date

columns.name: expiryDate
columns.description: The date when the training or compliance activity expires.

business_rules: Can be null if the training does not have an expiration date
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for future dates if applicable
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the expiration date of a training or compliance activity, which is crucial for tracking learner performance and compliance requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see individual training expirations
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings expiring within a certain period

columns.description: Column loID

columns.name: userID
columns.description: Column userID

columns.name: isCompleted
columns.description: Column isCompleted

columns.name: isOverdue
columns.description: Column isOverdue

columns.description: Column score

columns.description: Column timeSpent

columns.description: Column satisfiedLate

columns.name: trainingStatus
columns.description: Column trainingStatus

columns.name: cancellationDate
columns.description: Column cancellationDate

columns.name: registrationDate
columns.description: Column registrationDate

columns.name: attemptStartDate
columns.description: Column attemptStartDate

columns.name: completionDate
columns.description: Column completionDate

columns.name: assignedDate
columns.description: Column assignedDate

columns.name: dueDate
columns.description: Column dueDate

columns.name: expiryDate
columns.description: Column expiryDate

Occasional write access for updates on training statuses and scores

filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to analyze individual attempts
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY date for daily reports

enhanced_columns.description: The date when the training or learning activity was completed by the user.
business_rules: Can be null if the training is not completed
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., cannot be after dueDate)
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date on which a learner has completed a specific training module or activity, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance in learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see individual completion dates

enhanced_columns.description: The date when the training or learning activity was assigned to the user.
business_rules: Can be null if the training has not been assigned yet
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for null values where applicable
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the date on which a specific training or learning activity was assigned to an employee, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance in learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by assignedDate to analyze training assignments over time
Filter by userID to see assignments for specific employees
aggregation_suggestions: GROUP BY assignedDate for trend analysis

enhanced_columns.description: The date by which a training or compliance activity must be completed.
business_rules: Can be null if the training is not time-sensitive
Must be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., registrationDate, completionDate)
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column represents the deadline for learners to complete their assigned training or compliance activities.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings due by date

enhanced_columns.description: The date when the training or compliance activity expires.
business_rules: Can be null if the training does not have an expiration date
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for future dates if applicable
enhanced_columns.business_description: This column indicates the expiration date of a training or compliance activity, which is crucial for tracking learner performance and compliance requirements.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userID to see individual training expirations
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of trainings expiring within a certain period

key_relationships: Links to employee records for userID to assess individual performance
Associates with training programs to evaluate course effectiveness
primary_use_cases: Generate performance reports for employee training completion
Analyze training effectiveness and identify areas for improvement
Monitor overdue training assignments and compliance status

semantic_description.description: The Transcript_csod table serves as a source for tracking the progress and outcomes of training sessions within a corporate learning management system. It captures essential details about user training activities, including completion status, scores, and relevant timestamps.

key_columns.name: loID
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each learning object or training module
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for linking training records to specific courses or modules, enabling detailed reporting and analysis.

key_columns.name: userID
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each user participating in the training
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for associating training records with individual users, allowing for personalized tracking and reporting.

key_columns.name: isCompleted
key_columns.description: Indicates whether the training module has been completed by the user
key_columns.business_significance: Helps in assessing user engagement and training effectiveness, as well as compliance with training requirements.

key_columns.name: score
key_columns.description: Score achieved by the user in the training module
key_columns.business_significance: Provides a quantitative measure of user performance, which can be used for evaluations and assessments.

key_columns.name: registrationDate
key_columns.description: Timestamp when the user registered for the training module
key_columns.business_significance: Useful for tracking user enrollment trends and understanding the timeline of training participation.

key_columns.name: completionDate
key_columns.description: Timestamp when the user completed the training module
key_columns.business_significance: Important for measuring the duration of training and for compliance reporting.

semantic_description.table_purpose: To maintain a comprehensive record of user training transcripts, including their progress, scores, and status updates, facilitating performance analysis and compliance tracking.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is integral to the operations of a corporate training program, enabling organizations to monitor employee training progress, assess training effectiveness, and ensure compliance with mandatory training requirements. It supports performance management and development initiatives by providing insights into user engagement and learning outcomes.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each training record is associated with a single user, allowing for comprehensive user profiles that include training history.
Each training module can have multiple records corresponding to different users, facilitating analysis of module effectiveness across the user base.

performance_considerations: Indexing on userID and completionDate for faster query performance
Regular maintenance to optimize data retrieval and storage efficiency

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

tables.name: Activity_csod
tables.description: This table contains detailed information about various learning activities within the Cornerstone OnDemand (CSOD) platform, including compliance, certification, and logistical details.

columns.name: loID
columns.description: Identifier for learning objects in the Activity_csod table

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of the learning activities
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format to ensure it meets VARCHAR standards
metadata.business_description: A unique identifier for each learning activity, used to track and manage learning performance within the Cornerstone system.

columns.name: activityName
columns.description: Name of the activity associated with learning or compliance.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the activity being described
data_quality_checks: Ensure the name is descriptive and relevant to the activity
Check for consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column represents the name of the learning activity or compliance task that employees are required to complete.

columns.name: activityCode
columns.description: A code representing a specific activity within the Cornerstone Learn system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should follow a predefined format if applicable
data_quality_checks: Check for valid activity codes against the defined list
Ensure no leading or trailing spaces
metadata.business_description: This column identifies the type of activity that learners can engage in, such as training sessions, compliance courses, or certifications.

columns.name: isCompliance
columns.description: Indicates whether the activity is related to compliance requirements.

business_rules: Can be null, indicating that compliance status is not applicable for some activities.
If true, the activity must be completed by learners to maintain compliance.
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are either true or false.
Check for null values where compliance is expected.
metadata.business_description: This column specifies if an activity is a compliance-related task that learners must complete to meet regulatory or organizational standards.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by activity type to see compliance-related activities

columns.name: activityType
columns.description: Type of activity associated with learning events in the system.

business_rules: Can be null
Must be a valid activity type defined in the system
data_quality_checks: Validate against predefined list of activity types
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column categorizes the type of learning activity, such as training, compliance, or certification, which is essential for understanding employee learning performance.

columns.name: estimatedDuration
columns.description: Estimated duration of the activity in hours or minutes.

business_rules: Can be null if the duration is not specified
Should be a positive numeric value if provided
data_quality_checks: Validate that the value is numeric if not null
Check for excessively high or low values that may indicate data entry errors
metadata.business_description: This column represents the expected time required for a learner to complete the activity, which is crucial for planning and scheduling training sessions.

columns.name: isCertification
columns.description: Indicates whether the activity is a certification program.

business_rules: Can be null
Values should be either 'Yes' or 'No' to indicate certification status
data_quality_checks: Check for valid values ('Yes', 'No')
Ensure no unexpected values are present
metadata.business_description: This column identifies if a specific learning activity is classified as a certification, which is crucial for compliance tracking and employee development.
aggregation_suggestions: SUM of estimated durations for certification activities

columns.name: isActive
columns.description: Indicates whether the activity is currently active or not.

business_rules: Can be null
Values must be 'true' or 'false'
data_quality_checks: Check for valid boolean values
Ensure no unexpected values are present
metadata.business_description: This column represents the active status of an activity in the Cornerstone Learn system, determining if the activity is available for learners.

columns.name: owner
columns.description: Column representing the individual or entity responsible for the activity.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the responsible party for the activity
data_quality_checks: Ensure that the owner is a valid reference to an existing entity
Check for consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: The owner of the activity, which could be a person or department responsible for managing or overseeing the activity.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by owner to analyze activities managed by specific individuals or departments

columns.name: cost
columns.description: Represents the cost associated with an activity in the Cornerstone Learn platform.

business_rules: Can be null if the activity is free
Should be a valid numeric value if provided
data_quality_checks: Ensure the value is a valid numeric format
Check for consistency in currency representation
metadata.business_description: The cost incurred for participating in a specific learning activity, which may include training sessions, certifications, or compliance activities.

columns.name: currency
columns.description: Column representing the currency type used for cost-related activities.

business_rules: Can be null if no cost is associated with the activity
Should conform to standard currency codes (e.g., USD, EUR)
data_quality_checks: Validate against a list of standard currency codes
Check for null values where cost is present
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the currency in which the costs of activities are denominated, allowing for financial analysis and reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by activities with associated costs

columns.name: subject
columns.description: Column representing the subject of the activity in the CSOD system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the subject of the activity
data_quality_checks: Ensure subject is descriptive and relevant to the activity
Check for consistency in subject naming conventions
metadata.business_description: The subject area or topic that the activity covers, which is essential for categorizing and filtering learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by subject to analyze specific learning topics
Use in conjunction with activityType for detailed reporting

columns.name: provider
columns.description: The entity or organization that offers the activity or course.

business_rules: Can be null if the activity is internal
Should be populated for external activities
data_quality_checks: Check for valid provider names
Ensure consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column represents the provider of the learning activity, which can be an internal department or an external vendor.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by provider to analyze specific activities
Filter by compliance status to see provider effectiveness

columns.name: instructor
columns.description: Name of the instructor responsible for the activity.

business_rules: Can be null if no instructor is assigned
Should be a valid name format
data_quality_checks: Check for empty values
Validate name format
metadata.business_description: The instructor who leads or facilitates the learning activity, providing guidance and support to learners.

columns.name: facility
columns.description: Column representing the facility where the activity takes place.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the facility name
data_quality_checks: Validate against a list of known facilities
Check for spelling errors
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the specific facility associated with a learning activity, which is important for tracking and reporting on employee learning performance.

columns.name: eventCode
columns.description: A code representing a specific event in the Activity table.

business_rules: Can be null
Should follow a predefined format if applicable
data_quality_checks: Check for valid event codes against a predefined list
Ensure no leading or trailing spaces
metadata.business_description: This column identifies the type of event associated with an activity, which can be used to categorize and analyze learning activities.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by eventCode to analyze specific events
Filter by isActive to see only current events

columns.name: activityStartDate
columns.description: The date when the activity is scheduled to start, represented as a string in timestamp format without time zone.

business_rules: Can be null if the activity has not been scheduled
Should follow the format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' if populated
data_quality_checks: Validate format to ensure it matches the expected timestamp format
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the start date of an activity within the Cornerstone Learn platform, which is essential for tracking and reporting on employee learning performance.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by activity type to analyze compliance activities
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of activities starting on a specific date
GROUP BY activityStartDate for trend analysis

columns.name: activityEndDate
columns.description: The date when the activity concludes, represented as a timestamp without time zone.

business_rules: Can be null if the activity is ongoing
Should be populated for completed activities
data_quality_checks: Ensure the date format is valid
Check for logical consistency with activityStartDate
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the end date of an activity, which is crucial for tracking compliance and performance in learning activities.
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of activities ending within a specific period
SUM of estimated durations for activities ending in a given timeframe

semantic_description.description: The Activity_csod table serves as a source repository for various activities, capturing essential details about each activity, including compliance status, duration, costs, and scheduling information.

key_columns.name: loID
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each activity
key_columns.business_significance: Acts as the primary key to uniquely identify and reference activities across the system.

key_columns.name: activityName
key_columns.description: Descriptive name of the activity
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for identifying and categorizing activities for reporting and analysis.

key_columns.name: activityCode
key_columns.description: Code associated with the activity for internal tracking
key_columns.business_significance: Facilitates quick reference and categorization of activities in operational processes.

key_columns.name: isCompliance
key_columns.description: Indicates if the activity is related to compliance requirements
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for ensuring that activities meet regulatory standards and organizational policies.

key_columns.name: estimatedDuration
key_columns.description: Estimated time required to complete the activity
key_columns.business_significance: Helps in planning and resource allocation for activities.

key_columns.name: cost
key_columns.description: Financial cost associated with the activity
key_columns.business_significance: Important for budgeting and financial analysis of activities.

key_columns.name: activityStartDate
key_columns.description: Scheduled start date of the activity
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for scheduling and timeline management of activities.

key_columns.name: activityEndDate
key_columns.description: Scheduled end date of the activity
key_columns.business_significance: Important for tracking the duration and completion of activities.

semantic_description.table_purpose: To provide a comprehensive overview of activities for compliance, certification, and operational management within an organization.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is utilized within the context of organizational activity management, particularly in sectors where compliance and certification are critical. It supports planning, execution, and monitoring of activities, ensuring that they align with business objectives and regulatory requirements.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Activities may involve multiple participants, and individuals can participate in various activities, necessitating a relationship for effective management.
Each activity may be owned or managed by a specific department, allowing for departmental accountability and resource management.

performance_considerations: Indexing on key columns such as activityCode and activityStartDate for faster query performance
Monitoring query performance to optimize data retrieval times for large datasets

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis
